title: leontodon infestus
intro: Leontodon infestus, also known as the sow thistle or wild artichoke, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which can help to relieve digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Leontodon infestus can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
